items.title: Fed Meeting Marked By Dissents
items.description: The FOMC voted today to cut the Fedâs five policy rates by 25 basis points, as widely expected, the third cut in 2025, after cutting by 100 basis points in 2024.

items.title: December FOMC: Fed Cuts Rates But Signals A Pause Ahead As Policy Inches Closer To Neutral
items.description: The Fed cut rates by 25 bps to 3.50â3.75%, marking a hawkish cut amid deep FOMC division and persistent uncertainty. Check out the market interpretations here.

items.title: Fed's Interest Rate Decision: December 10, 2025
items.description: The Federal Reserve concluded its last meeting of the year with a widely anticipated 25 basis point cut to the federal funds rate (FFR), bringing it to a range of 3.50-3.75%.

items.title: Another Fed Cut, But There Is Growing Dissent
items.description: The Federal Reserve has cut the Fed funds target rate range 25bp to 3.5-3.75% as widely expected.

items.title: Fed Watch: Recalibrating The 'Recalibration'
items.description: With a third consecutive rate cut bringing the Fed Funds range to 3.50â3.75%, the Fed may pause for now as it reassesses the effectiveness of its ârisk managementâ approach.
